source_paper_title: Two Typical Implementable Semismooth* Newton Methods for Generalized Equations Are G-Semismooth Newton Methods
context:
Page 20, Section 6 (Conclusions)

Accordingly, a natural question is whether an implementable semismooth* Newton method is achievable for solving a GE that cannot be reformulated to locally Lipschitz continuous equations.
title: Implementable semismooth\(*\) Newton method for generalized equations not reducible to locally Lipschitz equations
latex:
Given integers \(n,m\ge 1\), let \(\Phi: \mathbb{R}^n \rightrightarrows \mathbb{R}^m\) be a set-valued mapping with closed graph \(\mathrm{gph}\,\Phi:=\{(x,y)\in\mathbb{R}^n\times\mathbb{R}^m\mid y\in\Phi(x)\}\). Consider the generalized equation
\[
0\in \Phi(x).
\]
Assume \(\bar x\in\mathbb{R}^n\) is a solution, i.e., \(0\in\Phi(\bar x)\). Assume further that there does not exist any neighborhood \(U\) of \(\bar x\) and any single-valued mapping \(H:U\to\mathbb{R}^m\) that is locally Lipschitz continuous on \(U\) and satisfies
\[
\{x\in U\mid 0\in\Phi(x)\}=\{x\in U\mid H(x)=0\}.
\]
Problem: develop an implementable semismooth\(*\) Newton-type algorithm for solving \(0\in\Phi(x)\) locally around \(\bar x\) (in particular, with tractable per-iteration subproblems such as linear systems) together with verifiable assumptions ensuring its local (superlinear) convergence, despite the absence of a reformulation to a locally Lipschitz continuous equation.
open_problem_quote: Accordingly, a natural question is whether an implementable semismooth* Newton method is achievable for solving a GE that cannot be reformulated to locally Lipschitz continuous equations.

verification.notice: This entry was generated from the paper text only. It has not been independently verified as still open. If you believe the question is solved or incorrectly stated, please contact the maintainers.
